page: Microsoft Azure B8pls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bpsv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The configuration includes 8 physical cores and 8 vCPUs, meaning there is no hyperthreading with exactly 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, though it is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks place the Microsoft Azure B8pls_v2 in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations, though single-core metrics lean toward the weaker end. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is recorded at 1822.39, while multi-core performance reaches 14490.19. Geekbench testing yields a single-core score of 1100.0 and a multi-core score of 6176.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 565.61 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 88.38 GB/sec for uncached large block reads and 47.82 GB/sec for large block writes. Passmark testing rates the overall CPU score at 6247.83 and the memory score at 1748.77, both falling into the lower performance tier.

Given its burstable CPU allocation and low memory-to-core ratio, the Microsoft Azure B8pls_v2 is designed for workloads with fluctuating CPU demands that do not require sustained high-performance compute or massive memory footprints. It achieves average performance in database operations with 5726.95 on Passmark and static web serving throughput of 10.06 GB/sec, making it suitable for lightweight web servers, development environments, and small database instances. However, its weak performance in cryptography and LLM inference makes it less suitable for intensive security or machine learning tasks. The burstable model provides a cost-efficient profile for intermittent workloads, trading off sustained performance for lower resource costs.
description: Microsoft Azure B8pls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Bpsv2 family, built on the ARM64 architecture with an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It features 8 vCPUs across 8 physical cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage or GPU. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance offers a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6176.0 and a static web serving throughput of 10.06 GB/sec, alongside weaker single-core and memory performance. The burstable CPU allocation makes it a cost-efficient option for workloads with variable CPU utilization, such as development environments, small database operations, and lightweight web serving, while avoiding the higher costs of sustained-performance instances.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B8pls_v2 is an ARM64 burstable virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and an Ampere Altra processor, offering a cost-efficient profile for variable workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B8pls_v2 is an ARM64 burstable instance with 8 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, optimized for low-memory, variable workloads.
tagline: A burstable ARM64 virtual machine offering balanced multi-core performance and low memory density for cost-sensitive, variable workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
Features 16.0 GB of RAM with a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU
Burstable CPU allocation designed for variable and intermittent workloads
Delivers average performance for static web serving and database operations
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on external premium storage
Best suited for development environments, lightweight web servers, and small databases
